files_modified.purpose: Package marker for integration test directory
Integration tests for full init cycle, performance gates, and idempotency

commit_subject: test(T004): add integration tests for init cycle and performance
commit_body: Add 6 integration tests across 4 scenarios: full init cycle structural validation (config.toml TOML parsing, session.json schema, constitution marker resolution, CLAUDE.md governance block), wall-clock performance under 500ms for both first-run and idempotent second-run, and pre-scaffolded directory idempotency verification. All 6 tests enforce the L_max <= 500ms performance constraint from US-006-PERF.

validation.summary: 6/6 integration tests passed, ruff lint clean, ruff format clean, 22/22 all-tests pass (448ms)

reasoning.approach: Created integration test package with 6 tests across 4 scenarios: full cycle validation, TOML/JSON structural checks, first-run and idempotent performance gates, and pre-scaffolded directory idempotency

key_decisions.decision: Split structural validation into 3 separate tests (full cycle, TOML, JSON)
key_decisions.rationale: Keeps each assertion focused: one test for file existence + governance, one for TOML deserialization content, one for JSON schema. Easier to diagnose failures.

key_decisions.decision: Used time.perf_counter() for performance tests per spec
key_decisions.rationale: perf_counter() provides monotonic high-resolution wall-clock timing suitable for sub-second measurements per US-006-PERF

key_decisions.decision: Pre-scaffolded test uses CLAUDE.md with existing governance block
key_decisions.rationale: Aligns with _upsert_governance_block behavior: file without the block gets appended, file with the block gets section-replaced. The test verifies content-aware idempotency.
